name: White Chicken Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 cups white beans, cooked and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until softened and lightly browned, about 5 to 8 minutes. Add the garlic, oregano, cumin, and ginger; cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
2. Add the broth, white wine, and bay leaf. Cook uncovered over medium-high heat until somewhat reduced, about 5 to 8 minutes.
3. Add the shredded chicken, beans, and jalapeno peppers. Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Using the back of a spoon, mash about one-quarter of the beans to thicken the sauce (can be made up to two days in advance and refrigerated or frozen; reheat gently before finishing).
4. Over low heat, add the cheese by handfuls, stirring until melted. Add the black pepper and taste for salt; add more if necessary.
5. Remove pieces of bay leaf. Serve the chili topped with some or all of the garnishes.
